informal: Prove that if $H$ is a subgroup of $G$ then $H$ is generated by the set $H-\{1\}$.
lean:
theorem Dummit_Foote_exercise_2_4_4_corrected {G : Type*} [Group G] (H : Subgroup G) :
    Subgroup.closure ((H : Set G) \ {1}) = H
reasoning: The informal statement says: for any subgroup H of G, H equals the subgroup generated by H \ {1}. The Lean signature takes an arbitrary group G and an arbitrary subgroup H, then asserts Subgroup.closure ((H : Set G) \ {1}) = H. "Subgroup.closure S" in Mathlib is precisely the subgroup generated by S, and (H : Set G) \ {1} is the underlying set of H minus the identity, matching "H − {1}". The quantification over G and H is captured by the universally quantified parameters, and no extra hypotheses (finiteness, abelian, etc.) are added. Hence the formalization is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
